dashboard.title: Anchorr Configuration
dashboard.subtitle: Anchorr needs to be connected with your Discord bot and with your Jellyfin and Seerr instances.
dashboard.subtitle_detail: Below is a step-by-step walkthrough to help you set up everything you need to get started!

config.title: Configuration
config.language_help: Select your preferred language for the interface.
config.discord_instructions_intro: First, you need to create a Discord application for the bot. Access Discord Developer Portal and log in. Create a new application, then:
config.discord_instructions_1: Go to "Bot" in the sidebar and click "Add Bot"
config.discord_instructions_2: In the "Bot" section, scroll down to "Privileged Gateway Intents" and enable SERVER MEMBERS INTENT (required for user mappings feature)
config.discord_instructions_6: Copy the generated URL, open it in your browser, and authorize the bot on your Discord server
config.discord_instructions_7: Come back here and fill in the Discord Bot Token and Bot Client ID below, save your settings, start the bot, and then select your server and notification channel from the dropdowns
config.discord_token_help: The secret token of your bot from the Discord Developer Portal → Bot → Reset Token
config.bot_id_help: The application's client ID from the Discord Developer Portal → OAuth2 → Client ID
config.next_step_1: Click Save Settings button at the bottom of the sidebar
config.next_step_2: Click the Start Bot button at the top of the page
config.next_step_3: Once the bot is running, the dropdowns below will automatically populate with your servers and channels
config.auto_start_info: Auto-Start: The bot will automatically start when you enter and save Discord credentials for the first time.
config.discord_server_help: Select the Discord server where the bot will operate. Make sure you've added the bot to your server first.
config.episode_channel_help: Optional: Choose a different channel specifically for episode notifications. Leave empty to use the default channel.
config.season_channel_help: Optional: Choose a different channel specifically for season notifications. Leave empty to use the default channel.
config.default_channel_help: Select the default Discord channel for Jellyfin notifications. You can override this per-library in Step 4. Choose a server above first.
config.seerr_url_help: The full URL of your Seerr instance.
config.seerr_api_key_help: Your API key from Seerr settings
config.default_quality_profiles_help: Set default quality profiles and servers for media requests. These will be used when users don't specify a profile via the /request command.
config.default_tv_quality_help: Quality profile for TV show requests (Sonarr)
config.default_tv_server_help: Sonarr server for TV show requests
config.default_anime_quality_help: Quality profile for anime TV series (Sonarr). Falls back to TV default if unset.
config.default_anime_movie_quality_help: Quality profile for anime movies (Radarr). Falls back to movie default if unset.
config.default_anime_server_help: Sonarr server for anime TV series. Falls back to TV server if unset.
config.default_anime_movie_server_help: Radarr server for anime movies. Falls back to movie server if unset.
config.tmdb_api_key_help: A TMDB API Key is required for searching and fetching movie/show details. You can find it in the API settings page of your TMDB account after you log in.
config.omdb_api_key_help: Optional, but recommended for richer data like ratings and directors. Create one on the API Key page of OMDb.
config.jellyfin_url_help: The public URL of your Jellyfin server. Used for the "Watch Now" button.
config.jellyfin_api_key_help: API key for your Jellyfin server. You can generate one in Jellyfin Dashboard → Administration → API Keys. This is required for loading libraries and enhanced features.
config.test_endpoint_help: Testing the endpoint successfully will automatically populate the Jellyfin Server ID field.
config.jellyfin_server_id_help: Used for redirecting to the correct path of your Jellyfin server media page when using the "Watch Now" button. You can find this in your Jellyfin URL when viewing the page of any movie or show from your library.
config.notification_testing_help: Test your notification templates by sending sample notifications to your Discord channel. This helps you preview how different types of notifications will appear.
config.debounce_help: Determines how long to wait before sending notifications for multiple episodes/seasons, bundling them into a single notification message.
config.library_channel_mapping_help: Map specific Jellyfin libraries to different Discord channels. Click "Fetch Libraries" to load your libraries, then select a channel for each one. Unchecked libraries will not send any notifications.
config.embed_customization_help: Customize which elements appear in your Discord notification embeds.
config.mappings_pane_description: Map Discord users to their Seerr accounts. When a user makes a request from Discord, it will be associated with their Seerr account instead of the API key owner.
config.discord_user_help: Select a Discord server member, or enter their User ID manually below if they don't appear in the list.
config.discord_user_id_placeholder: Or enter Discord User ID manually...
config.roles_pane_description: Control who can use bot commands by configuring role-based permissions. If no roles are selected in the allowlist, everyone can use commands. If any role is added to the blocklist, members with that role will be blocked regardless of allowlist settings.
config.allowlist_help: If empty, all members can use commands. If roles are selected, only members with these roles can use commands.
config.blocklist_help: Members with these roles will never be able to use bot commands, even if they have an allowed role.
config.misc_pane_description: Optional settings to enhance your bot experience. These are extra features that can be enabled after your bot is fully configured and running.
config.auto_start_help: If enabled, the bot starts automatically when the container/server restarts and valid Discord credentials are present.
config.notify_on_available_label: Send PM when request is available
config.notify_on_available_help: When enabled, users will receive a private message when their requested content becomes available on Jellyfin.
config.ephemeral_mode_help: When enabled, all bot responses are sent as ephemeral messages (only visible to the user who used the command). When disabled, successful results are visible to everyone in the channel.
config.auto_approve_help: When enabled, requests made through the bot will be automatically approved in Seerr. If disabled, requests will remain "Pending" for manual approval.
config.daily_pick_help: Send a daily recommendation of random media to a Discord channel. Great for discovering hidden gems!
config.daily_pick_channel_help: Discord channel where daily picks will be sent
config.daily_pick_interval_help: How often to send a random pick in minutes (default: 1440 = 24 hours)
config.weekly_roundup_help: Post a weekly digest of new Jellyfin content to a Discord channel. Runs once per week on the configured weekday and hour.
config.weekly_roundup_channel_help: Discord channel where the weekly roundup will be posted
config.weekly_roundup_color_help: Hex color for the roundup embed. Leave empty to use the default series color.
config.weekly_roundup_role_help: Optional. Pings the selected role when the roundup posts. Bot must be running to load the role list.
config.embed_colors_help: Customize the colors of Discord notification embeds for different content types.

search.description: Search for a movie/TV show (you can request it later)
search.error: An error occurred while processing your request.

request.description: Instantly send a request for a movie/TV show

trending.description: Browse trending movies and TV shows

errors.loading_members_bot_running: Error loading members. Is bot running?
errors.bot_must_be_running: Bot must be running to load roles
errors.language_load_failed: UI language could not be loaded. Some labels may be missing.
errors.autostart_check: Warning: Could not check bot autostart state. Saving anyway.
errors.copy_url_failed: Failed to copy URL. Please copy manually.
errors.jellyfin_api_key_required: Please enter Jellyfin API Key first.
errors.mapping_refresh_failed: Mappings updated, but failed to refresh the list. Please reload the page.
errors.discord_user_lookup_failed: Failed to look up Discord user.
errors.refresh_users_failed: Failed to refresh users. Check connections.

user_mapping.select_users: Please select both a Discord user and a Seerr user.

about.title: About Anchorr
about.description: Anchorr is a self-hosted, lightweight bridge that connects your Jellyfin media server with Discord. It notifies you on a dedicated channel when new media is added to your library, and with Seerr integration, you can search and request movies and shows directly from Discord.
about.feature_search: /search <title> Search for a movie or show with auto-suggestions. An embed with detailed information is shown, from which you can request the media. For TV shows, you have the option to select the desired seasons for the request.
about.feature_request: /request <title> Instantly send a request for your chosen movie or show directly to Seerr. For TV shows, this automatically requests all seasons.
about.feature_notifications: Receive beautifully formatted notifications on your selected Discord channel whenever new media is added to your Jellyfin library.
about.feature_databases: Using the TMDB and OMDb databases, Discord messages get a beautiful and well-structured look, with essential information about the requested or added media content.
about.feature_permissions: Control permissions with role-based access (allowlist/blocklist), route notifications to different channels by Jellyfin library, and link users to Seerr accounts for accurate request tracking.
about.feature_direct_messages: Get direct messages on Discord when requested content becomes available in your library. Never miss when your requests are ready!
about.support_description: If you find Anchorr useful, consider supporting its development. Every coffee helps fuel late-night coding sessions!
about.contribute_description: Anchorr is open-source! If you're a developer, your contributions are welcome. Check out the project on GitHub to get started.

jellyfin_webhook.note: If you're running Anchorr in Docker, you may need to replace localhost with your server's IP address.
jellyfin_webhook.plugin_install: If you don't have the plugin installed, access the Plugins page in your Jellyfin Dashboard, search for Webhook, install it and restart the server.
jellyfin_webhook.plugin_setup: After the restart, access your Webhook plugin settings and Add Generic Destination.
jellyfin_webhook.step_server_url: Complete the Server Url with your Jellyfin server's base URL.
jellyfin_webhook.step_webhook_url: Insert the previously specified URL in the Webhook Url field.
jellyfin_webhook.step_notification_type: For Notification Type enable Item Added.
jellyfin_webhook.step_item_type: For Item Type select your desired content you want to receive notifications for.
jellyfin_webhook.step_headers: Scroll down to the Headers section and click Add Header. Set the name to X-Webhook-Secret and paste your copied secret as the value.
jellyfin_webhook.generic_destination_notice: Jellyfin does not support custom headers for the Discord Destination type. If you previously used a Discord Destination, you need to delete it and recreate it as a Generic Destination — otherwise the X-Webhook-Secret header cannot be added and Anchorr will reject all incoming requests.
jellyfin_webhook.webhook_secret_desc: Your webhook secret is used to secure incoming requests. Copy it below — you'll need to add it as a custom HTTP header in the Jellyfin webhook plugin:
jellyfin_webhook.notification_testing_info: These tests bypass the webhook endpoint and run internally — they work without the X-Webhook-Secret header. Make sure you've still copied the secret and added it to your Jellyfin webhook plugin so that real notifications from Jellyfin are accepted.
